seed_problem: ALE-Bench pickup-delivery TSP (ahc006/027/039); Frontier-CS routing: choose which precedence-constrained pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance.
mutation: composite: quota-constrained selective pickup-and-delivery single-vehicle TSP with drawbar (capacity) batching -- REPLACE the additive skip-penalty term with a hard coverage quota (complete at least M of N precedence-constrained orders) and minimize the pure closed depot-to-depot Manhattan tour length; skinned as a night-shift shunting locomotive in a railway freight yard. Subset choice, capacity-limited batching of cuts, and tour ordering interact so value-blind, cheapest-solo greedy and batched nearest-neighbor/local-search strategies diverge.
title: Night-Shift Shunting in the Riverbend Freight Yard
strategies: serve-everything one-cut-at-a-time in input order: reproduces the checker baseline B, ratio = 0.1 (calibration point)
cheapest-solo greedy: complete the M orders with the smallest solo round-trip cost, one cut at a time in cost order -- drops the far/expensive orders but never batches
batched nearest-neighbor: on the chosen served set, repeatedly take the nearest feasible pickup (if the drawbar limit allows) or delivery, carrying several cuts at once to amortize deadhead moves

insertion + capacity-aware local search: cheapest-insertion of profitable orders respecting precedence and the drawbar limit, then or-opt / 2-opt reordering, alternating with re-selection of the served subset
LP/assignment-relaxation lower bounds and matching-based stacker-crane heuristics to guide subset and sequence choice
